req.title: Device data ingest (boundary)
req.statement: A registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest; unregistered devices shall be rejected. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct test dimensions: (1) a registered device successfully pushing a valid FHIR Observation (EP positive path), (2) an unregistered/invalid-cert device being rejected with HTTP 401 (NEG), and (3) boundary values on timestamp and numeric elements such as the minimum/maximum allowed numeric value and an extreme timestamp at epoch boundary (BVA). Additional coverage uses Decision Table reasoning to verify normalization is applied when the observation is stored, and State Transition Coverage to confirm the device moves from 'submitted' to 'stored' state only when registered. Each test posts to /Ingest and asserts the HTTP response code and body content.

tests.name: test_registered_device_ingest_valid_observation
preconditions: Device is registered in fhir-lite device registry
Valid TLS client certificate is available via <registered_cert_fixture>
FHIR Observation payload with a normal numeric value and current ISO-8601 timestamp is prepared
actions: Build a valid FHIR Observation JSON body with resourceType='Observation', status='final', effectiveDateTime=now(), valueQuantity.value=98.6
POST the payload to /Ingest using the registered device certificate
Capture HTTP response status code and body
expected: Response body contains a FHIR OperationOutcome or Observation resource with id populated
Stored Observation is retrievable via GET /Observation/{id}
Normalization flag or normalized unit is present in the stored resource
trace: R-HLT-047: registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/Ingest"

@pytest.fixture
def registered_cert():
    return <registered_cert_fixture>

@pytest.fixture
def valid_observation():
    return <build_fhir_observation>(value=98.6, timestamp=<utc_now_iso8601>())

def test_registered_device_ingest_valid_observation(registered_cert, valid_observation):
    resp = requests.post(
        BASE_URL,
        json=valid_observation,
        cert=registered_cert,
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"},
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    obs_id = body.get("id") or (body.get("entry", [{}])[0].get("resource", {}).get("id"))
    assert obs_id, "No Observation id returned"
    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"http://fhir-lite/Observation/{obs_id}",
        cert=registered_cert,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    stored = get_resp.json()
    assert stored.get("resourceType") == "Observation"
    assert <normalization_applied>(stored), "Normalization not applied"

tests.name: test_unregistered_device_cert_rejected_with_401
preconditions: An invalid or unregistered TLS client certificate is available via <unregistered_cert_fixture>
A syntactically valid FHIR Observation payload is prepared
actions: Build a syntactically valid FHIR Observation JSON body
POST the payload to /Ingest using the unregistered/invalid device certificate
Capture HTTP response status code and body
expected: Response body indicates authentication failure (e.g. OperationOutcome with issue.code='security')
No Observation resource is created in the data store
trace: R-HLT-047: unregistered devices shall be rejected
R-HLT-047: error pathway 401 when device cert invalid

tests.name: test_boundary_timestamp_epoch_and_max_numeric_value
preconditions: Device is registered in fhir-lite device registry
Valid TLS client certificate is available via <registered_cert_fixture>
System accepts FHIR dateTime values at Unix epoch (1970-01-01T00:00:00Z)
System defines maximum allowed valueQuantity.value as per profile constraints
actions: Build a FHIR Observation with effectiveDateTime set to Unix epoch '1970-01-01T00:00:00Z'
Set valueQuantity.value to the maximum allowed boundary value (e.g. 999999.99)
POST the payload to /Ingest using the registered device certificate
Capture HTTP response status code and body
Also POST a payload with valueQuantity.value exceeding maximum (e.g. 1000000.00) and assert rejection
expected: Epoch timestamp observation returns 201 or 422 per profile rules (documented boundary)
Value exceeding maximum returns 422 Unprocessable Entity
Response body for rejection contains OperationOutcome with issue describing value constraint
trace: R-HLT-047: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/Ingest"
EPOCH_TS = "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z"
MAX_VALUE = 999999.99
OVER_MAX_VALUE = 1000000.00

@pytest.fixture
def registered_cert():
    return <registered_cert_fixture>

def test_boundary_timestamp_epoch_and_max_numeric_value(registered_cert):
    epoch_obs = <build_fhir_observation>(value=MAX_VALUE, timestamp=EPOCH_TS)
    resp_epoch = requests.post(
        BASE_URL,
        json=epoch_obs,
        cert=registered_cert,
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"},
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp_epoch.status_code in (201, 422), (
        f"Epoch TS: expected 201 or 422, got {resp_epoch.status_code}"
    )
    max_obs = <build_fhir_observation>(value=MAX_VALUE, timestamp=<utc_now_iso8601>())
    resp_max = requests.post(
        BASE_URL,
        json=max_obs,
        cert=registered_cert,
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"},
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp_max.status_code == 201, f"Max boundary value: expected 201, got {resp_max.status_code}"
    over_obs = <build_fhir_observation>(value=OVER_MAX_VALUE, timestamp=<utc_now_iso8601>())
    resp_over = requests.post(
        BASE_URL,
        json=over_obs,
        cert=registered_cert,
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"},
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp_over.status_code == 422, f"Over-max value: expected 422, got {resp_over.status_code}"

tests.name: test_device_state_transition_registered_to_stored
preconditions: Device is registered in fhir-lite device registry with status='active'
Valid TLS client certificate is available via <registered_cert_fixture>
No prior Observation exists for this device in the current test run
actions: Confirm device status is 'active' via GET /Device/{device_id}
POST a valid FHIR Observation to /Ingest using the registered certificate
Assert transition: Observation moves from 'submitted' to 'stored' state
Retrieve the Observation by returned id and verify status='final'
expected: Device remains in 'active' state after submission
Observation resource status is 'final' in the stored representation
GET /Observation/{id} returns the stored resource with status='final'
trace: R-HLT-047: registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite"
DEVICE_ID = <test_device_id>

@pytest.fixture
def registered_cert():
    return <registered_cert_fixture>

def test_device_state_transition_registered_to_stored(registered_cert):
    dev_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Device/{DEVICE_ID}",
        cert=registered_cert,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert dev_resp.status_code == 200
    assert dev_resp.json().get("status") == "active", "Device not active before test"
    obs = <build_fhir_observation>(value=75.0, timestamp=<utc_now_iso8601>())
    post_resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Ingest",
        json=obs,
        cert=registered_cert,
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"},
        timeout=10
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201, f"POST failed: {post_resp.status_code}"
    obs_id = post_resp.json().get("id")
    assert obs_id, "No id in POST response"
    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Observation/{obs_id}",
        cert=registered_cert,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json().get("status") == "final"

tests.name: test_decision_table_cert_and_registration_combinations
preconditions: Registered device cert is available via <registered_cert_fixture>
Unregistered device cert is available via <unregistered_cert_fixture>
A syntactically valid FHIR Observation payload is available
actions: Case A: registered cert + valid payload -> POST /Ingest -> expect 201
Case B: unregistered cert + valid payload -> POST /Ingest -> expect 401
Case C: no cert (anonymous) + valid payload -> POST /Ingest -> expect 401 or 403
Assert each case independently; record response codes
expected: No observation is stored for Cases B and C
trace: R-HLT-047: unregistered devices shall be rejected
R-HLT-047: error pathway 401 when device cert invalid
R-HLT-047: registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest
